reviewSummary.summary: The draft captures the core guest-state navigation controls required by the workflow, but it contains weak semantic naming, a duplicate generic link element, and a low-value background container element. The approved controls should be retained with improved business-oriented names and removal of noisy generic elements.

issues.message: The element name is too generic and loses the workflow-specific meaning of the Person/Profile control.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to person_profile_button to align with the workflow terminology and downstream reusable navigation intent.

issues.message: The element name lacks readability and business clarity because words are concatenated.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to customer_signup_button for better readability and downstream keyword generation.

issues.message: The element name is compressed and less reusable for automation assets.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to clean_survey_button to preserve business meaning and readability.

issues.message: The element name is compressed and less semantically meaningful for reusable automation.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to site_survey_button for consistency with workflow terminology.

issues.message: The generic xpath=//a locator represents a duplicate low-value wrapper anchor associated with the Home button shadow DOM and is too broad for reliable automation.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the generic link element and reuse the dedicated home_button control with locator id=auto_home_btn.

issues.message: The locator xpath=//a is overly generic and likely to match multiple links across the SPA.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this locator entirely or replace it with a uniquely identifiable business-specific locator if a dedicated link element is truly needed.

issues.message: The background content container is primarily a layout wrapper and does not provide strong business-level verification value for downstream automation.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the background_content element unless it is explicitly required as a stable page-load synchronization anchor.

issues.message: The underlying native control appears to be an anchor element inside the ion-button wrapper, but the approved type is button.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the high-level semantic control as home_button but document that it navigates like a link-backed navigation control within the SPA.

issues.message: The workflow requires validation that authenticated-only controls and logged-in username indicators are absent or non-actionable, but no observable negative-state indicator elements are modeled.
issues.suggested_fix: Add dedicated observable guest-state or authenticated-state indicator elements if available in the DOM to support stronger negative assertions instead of relying only on absence checks.

approved_elements.name: person_profile_button
approved_elements.description: Opens the Login page or Login view from the Home guest state.

approved_elements.name: back_button
approved_elements.description: Shared Home shell back navigation control available in guest state.

approved_elements.name: notification_button
approved_elements.description: Shared Home shell notification access control displayed in guest state.

approved_elements.name: home_button
approved_elements.description: Shared Home shell navigation control for returning to the Home page.

approved_elements.name: customer_signup_button
approved_elements.description: Navigates the guest user to the customer-search page.

approved_elements.name: clean_survey_button
